message:
Fix Kafka IAM metadata auth race and add deep refresh diagnostics

The neo from_kafka path could fail early with:
  failed to query topic metadata: Local: Broker transport failure
while aws_iam was configured.

Root cause
- metadata() was queried before librdkafka had serviced any OAUTHBEARER token
  refresh callback in some executions.
- In those runs, oauth refresh state remained attempts=0, so no token was
  installed yet, but the failure surfaced only as a generic transport error.
- Separately, aws_iam.profile used a provider that did not cover SSO profile
  resolution paths well enough.

Fixes
- Support SSO-backed named profiles for Kafka IAM profile mode in both callback
  implementations by using a profile provider chain:
  ProfileConfigFileAWSCredentialsProvider + SSOCredentialsProvider.
- Add shared oauth refresh diagnostics state to consumer_configuration and wire
  it into the neo oauth callback.
- Track callback attempts/successes/failures and last callback result/failure.
- In neo partition discovery, proactively poll briefly to serve oauth refresh
  callbacks before metadata query, and retry metadata once if no callback
  attempt happened yet.
- Extend metadata-failure diagnostics with oauth refresh state to clearly
  distinguish pre-auth callback starvation from credential/signing failures.

This keeps behavior deterministic while preserving detailed on-failure context
for hard-to-reproduce auth races.
